name: English Trifle- Guy Attwood
recipeIngredient: 1 to 2 small pound or sponge cakes (at least two days old)
3/4 cup sherry (preferably a good quality brand)
10 oz package of thawed and drained frozen raspberries
10 tbsp whole milk (1/2 cup plus 2 Tbsp)
2 1/2 tsp cornstarch dissolved in 2 Tbsp water
recipeInstructions:
This recipe was given to me by my grandmother, who immigrated from England in 1895. I have made a few minor adjustments. It is an indulgent and visually appealing dessert perfect for special occasions.
1. Whisk egg yolks in a medium saucepan until thickened.
2. Gradually add sugar, whisking constantly until the mixture becomes thick and lemon-colored (about 1-2 minutes).
3. Stir in milk, whipping cream, and cornstarch solution. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until thickened (about 3-5 minutes). Do not boil; otherwise, the mixture will separate.
4. Remove from heat and let cool slightly before adding vanilla extract and nutmeg. Repeat for all required servings.
Assembly:
1. In a large crystal bowl, spread about half of the whipped cream evenly on the bottom.
2. Scatter pieces of cake throughout the cream.
3. Drizzle sherry over the top, followed by dollops of jam and raspberries (about half of the drained raspberries).
4. Pour half of the custard over everything.
5. Repeat layers, reserving some raspberries for the top.
6. Finish with a layer of jam, raspberries, and drizzle chocolate on top.
Cover tightly and refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving. Serve in a pretty bowl.
